'''Stuart Reardon''' (born 13 October 1981) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|fb|wg}}, or {{rlp|ce}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> An [[England national rugby league team|England]] and [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] international, he played for [[Bradford Bulls]], [[Warrington Wolves]] and [[North Wales Crusaders]], and also had loan spells with [[Featherstone Rovers]] and [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] during his early career.

==Playing career==
===Club===
Reardon turned professional from [[West Bowling A.R.L.F.C.]] (in [[Little Horton|West Bowling]], [[Bradford]]) to sign for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in 2000. He was loaned out to [[Featherstone Rovers]] at the start of the 2002 season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Great expectations for Noble's future |url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/news/8034400.great-expectations-for-nobles-future/ |website=Bradford Telegraph and Argus |access-date=24 January 2026 |date=8 December 2001}}</ref> When former Bradford Bulls assistant coach [[Karl Harrison]] took over as [[head coach]] of the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]], he signed Reardon on loan to boost the Salford City Reds' fight against relegation.<ref>{{cite web |title=Salford take Reardon |url=https://newsimg.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/2168980.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=24 January 2026 |date=2 August 2002}}</ref>

He enjoyed a rapid rise from Academy to Super League and Test rugby league between 2002 and 2004.

He returned to the Bradford Bulls in 2003 and found a place in the first team after a long term injury to [[Michael Withers]], making 20 appearances in the title-winning Bradford Bulls team, including being {{rlp|fb}} in the [[2003 Super League Grand Final]] against [[Wigan Warriors]] in which he scored a [[Try (rugby)|try]]. Reardon scored a try and won the [[Harry Sunderland Trophy]] for his [[Player of the match|man of the match]] performance at the [[Old Trafford]] showdown.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ex-Great Britain international Stuart Reardon signs for North Wales Crusaders |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/8173063/ex-great-britain-international-stuart-reardon-signs-for-north-wales-crusaders |access-date=2023-12-01 |website=Sky Sports |language=en}}</ref> Having won [[Super League VIII]], Bradford played against 2003 NRL Premiers, the [[Penrith Panthers]] in the [[2004 World Club Challenge]]. Reardon played from the interchange bench in the Bradford Bulls' 22–4 victory. Later that year Reardon played for the Bradford Bulls as a {{rlp|wg}} in their [[2004 Super League Grand Final]] loss against the [[Leeds Rhinos]]. He missed the back end of 2005's [[Super League X]] with injury. 

In September 2005, Reardon turned down a new contract with Bradford and joined [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Warrington sign Reardon & Barnett |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/warrington/4224200.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=24 January 2026 |date=7 September 2005}}</ref> Paul Cullen described as the 'best kick returner in Super League' during 2006's [[Super League XI]], Reardon was the Warrington Wolves leading tackle-buster with 66 in his first year at the club. 

Reardon joined [[Hull F.C.|Hull]] in April 2009, but was released 11 days later after a failed medical.<ref>{{cite web |title=Reardon leaves Hull after 11 days |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/8013608.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=24 January 2026 |date=22 April 2009}}</ref> He re-joined his former club Bradford in October 2009.<ref>{{cite web |title=Reardon in surprise Bulls return |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/bradford/8315839.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=24 January 2026 |date=20 October 2009}}</ref>

He won the double with [[AS Carcassonne]] in France in 2012 – ( French Elite League champions and French Elite Cup winners ) He won the double in 2013 with [[North Wales Crusaders]] – ( Championship 1 champions and Championship bowl cup winners. )

===International===
Reardon earned a call up to the England 'A' tour of Fiji and Tonga in 2002, where he worked with [[Warrington Wolves]] coach [[Paul Cullen (rugby league)|Paul Cullen]]. Reardon represented [[England national rugby league team#Other England Teams|England 'A']] while at the Bradford Bulls in the [[2003 European Nations Cup]] against Wales and France. 

He broke into the Great Britain squad for the [[2004 Rugby League Tri-Nations|2004 Tri Nations series]],<ref>{{cite web |title=Unflappable Reardon rises to the occasion |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/international_and_australian/4035075.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=24 January 2026 |date=24 November 2004}}</ref> and scored four tries in the tournament. Reardon won [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] while at the Bradford Bulls in 2004 against Australia (3-matches), and New Zealand (2-matches), and won [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[England national rugby league team|England]] while at the Bradford Bulls in 2005 against France, and New Zealand ([[Rugby league positions#Interchange|interchange]]/[[Substitution (sport)|substitute]]). 

He made the England team at the end of the 2005 season, and toured Australia and New Zealand as a stand-by for the Great Britain squad in 2006. 

==Modelling and Training career==
After ending his rugby league career, Reardon worked as a [[fitness model (person)|fitness model]].<ref name="I’ve got a good body so I’m happy to get my kit off says England and GB rugby player Stuart Reardon">{{cite web|url=http://www.liverpoolecho.co.uk/liverpool-life/fashion/2011/06/29/i-ve-got-a-good-body-so-i-m-happy-to-get-my-kit-off-says-england-and-gb-rugby-player-stuart-reardon-100252-28958954|title="I've got a good body so I'm happy to get my kit off" says England and GB rugby player Stuart Reardon|publisher=liverpoolecho.co.uk|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref><ref name="Rugby Player Stuart Reardon">{{cite web|url=http://rorylewisphotography.com/blog/rugby-player-stuart-reardon|title=Rugby Player Stuart Reardon|publisher=rorylewisphotography.com|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref>

Reardon is the face of Axiom for Men, a men's grooming product range.<ref>{{cite web|title=AXIOM FOR MEN|url=http://www.bangandstrike.com/bangtalk/axiom-for-men|publisher=bangandstrike.com|accessdate=23 August 2017|date=28 February 2014}}</ref> He also appears in many magazines including [[DNA (magazine)|DNA]], [[Muscle & Fitness (British magazine)|Muscle & Fitness]], Gloss, Q Vegas, EILE Magazine and many more. He's worked with photographers in Los Angeles, Las Vegas, New York and Great Britain.

Stuart runs an online training program, Fear Nothing Fitness, featuring body weight workout videos.

He is also a published author with Jane Harvey-Berrick, and his contemporary romance and romantic comedies are published in seven languages.
